meta.value_prop: PSI + cosine + entropy drift detection. 3-step API. Webhook alerts. 10 free checks/day. $0.01 USDC/check.
meta.free_tier: 3 models per IP, 10 drift checks/day, 1 baseline reset/model/day

targets.name: Chip Huyen
targets.domain: MLOps education, author of 'Designing Machine Learning Systems' (O'Reilly)
targets.pain_point: Chapter 8 of her book covers data distribution shifts in depth — she knows how hard it is to detect and act on in production. She's heard every complaint about drift monitoring being too complex.
targets.personalization_hook: Your chapter 8 on distribution shifts is the most dog-eared section of my copy — the gap between 'PSI > 0.25' and 'here's a working endpoint to check it' is exactly what I built.
targets.context: Extremely high signal audience. A single retweet from her could drive hundreds of signups. Treat this as a top-tier cold pitch.
targets.notes: Active on Twitter. Has mentioned wanting simpler tools. Do NOT ask her to try it blindly — give her the 3-line curl example upfront.

targets.name: Hamel Husain
targets.domain: LLM engineering, evals, fine-tuning pipelines, W&B advisor
targets.pain_point: Writes and podcasts constantly about LLM output quality degradation. His core thesis: most teams discover model rot from angry users, not metrics. Wants proactive monitoring.
targets.personalization_hook: You've said monitoring LLM outputs is the biggest gap in the eval toolchain. The drift API tracks text-stat drift (length z-score + vocab diversity) — send it baseline outputs, get a score.
targets.context: Heavy on substack/newsletter. Engaged community. He responds to technical pitches that show actual output examples.
targets.notes: Best approach: include a working drift check example with LLM outputs. Show text model_type in the curl.

targets.name: Shreya Shankar
targets.domain: ML monitoring research, UC Berkeley PhD, co-authored 'Rethinking Data Monitoring'
targets.pain_point: Her research shows practitioners discover drift weeks late because monitoring tooling is too heavyweight to actually deploy. She's looking for lightweight, deployable solutions.
targets.personalization_hook: Your paper 'Rethinking Data Monitoring' describes exactly why I built this — the gap between knowing PSI and having a /check endpoint your CI can hit. 3 endpoints, no infra.
targets.context: Academic + practitioner hybrid. Very technical. Engage her as a peer — cite the paper, show the algorithm (PSI for numeric, cosine for embeddings). She may want to experiment.
targets.notes: Could become a genuine user AND help validate the approach academically. Treat like a collab pitch, not a sales pitch.

targets.name: Eugene Yan
targets.domain: Applied ML at Amazon, recommendation systems, writing at eugeneyan.com
targets.pain_point: Has written about recommendation system staleness and the challenge of knowing when a model's predictions have drifted from what users actually click. Scoring drift is his exact use case.
targets.personalization_hook: You wrote about recsys drift in 'ML beyond curation' — the PSI method in this API is exactly what you described wanting to automate: bucket the output score distribution, compare to baseline.
targets.context: Practical, builder mindset. He values tools he can drop into a pipeline. Show the webhook integration angle — he cares about operationalizing, not just detecting.
targets.notes: Has a large newsletter audience. If he writes about the tool, it reaches 20k+ ML practitioners.

targets.name: swyx
targets.domain: AI developer, Latent Space podcast, 'AI engineer' community builder
targets.pain_point: Building AI products that depend on LLM APIs. When OpenAI/Anthropic silently updates model behavior, apps break silently. Wants early warning when outputs shift.
targets.personalization_hook: Every time a model provider does a silent update, your users notice before you do. This catches it: baseline your app's outputs this week, get a webhook the moment distributions shift.
targets.context: Very community-oriented. Could share in AI Engineer Discord/Slack. Pitch the 'silent model update' angle hard — it resonates with AI app builders.
targets.notes: Keep pitch punchy. He gets tons of pitches. Lead with the problem statement, not the API docs.

targets.name: Simon Willison
targets.domain: Datasette, LLM tools, prolific builder, writes daily at simonwillison.net
targets.pain_point: Runs many side LLM experiments and tools. No systematic way to know if an experiment's output distribution is changing over time. Probably just checks manually.
targets.personalization_hook: For any LLM experiment you're running — send baseline outputs to /drift/register + /drift/baseline, then check daily. Three curl commands and you have a time-series of model stability.
targets.context: Bluesky native. Very responsive to interesting tools. He blogs about everything he finds interesting — getting a mention on his blog is enormous.
targets.notes: Do NOT pitch revenue. Pitch the tool as interesting engineering. Show the text model_type. He'll want to poke at the API.

targets.name: Harrison Chase
targets.domain: LangChain founder/CEO, LLM orchestration, massive developer community
targets.pain_point: LangChain pipelines use LLMs as components. When the underlying model updates, the pipeline output distribution shifts but there's no native monitoring. LangSmith doesn't do statistical drift.
targets.personalization_hook: LangSmith tracks traces. This tracks whether your output *distribution* is changing — probability entropy + KL divergence on your chain's outputs. Complementary, not redundant.
targets.context: High profile. Low response probability unless pitch is exceptionally differentiated. Best angle: LangSmith integration story — 'this adds the missing stat layer.'
targets.notes: Pitch the embedding model_type — LangChain users have embeddings everywhere. Show how cosine drift on embedding outputs catches semantic shift.

targets.name: Vicki Boykis
targets.domain: ML engineer, staff engineer at Mozilla, writes 'Normcore Tech' newsletter
targets.pain_point: Consistently writes that production ML is mostly unglamorous reliability work. Has complained about monitoring tooling being either too complex (full Evidently stack) or too naive (just log accuracy).
targets.personalization_hook: You've written that real ML engineering is 'maintaining things that break quietly.' PSI > 0.25 on your score distribution is the thing that breaks quietly — this makes it loud.
targets.context: Respected, opinionated voice. Very skeptical of hype. Pitch must be direct and honest — what it does, what it doesn't do, exact pricing. She'll respect that.
targets.notes: Do NOT oversell. She will call it out publicly. Be precise about the algorithms (PSI, cosine, KL). She'll appreciate the numpy-only implementation.

targets.name: Linus Lee
targets.domain: Independent AI researcher, builder of experimental ML tools, Farcaster native
targets.pain_point: Runs many experimental ML models and tools (Mira, etc.). Output consistency over time matters but he has no systematic way to measure it. Likely just does vibes-based evaluation.
targets.personalization_hook: For any model you're iterating on — this gives you a number for 'has my output distribution changed?' Cosine drift on embeddings, PSI on numeric scores. API, no infra.
targets.context: Farcaster native. Technical, builder, values simplicity and elegance. Respond to aesthetically clean APIs. Show the full 3-step flow compactly.
targets.notes: Could become a genuine power user who writes about the tool publicly. Farcaster DM is right channel — he's active there.

targets.name: Tim Schuhmann
targets.domain: LAION founder, large-scale ML datasets, model quality tracking
targets.pain_point: LAION manages massive datasets used to train models. Tracking whether model outputs shift across dataset versions or fine-tunes is a core problem. Statistical drift detection at scale.
targets.personalization_hook: For LAION-trained models — send a sample of output embeddings as baseline, then check each new fine-tune checkpoint for cosine drift. Catches distribution shifts before your evals do.
targets.context: Very technical audience. Cares about scale and rigor. Pitch the embedding model_type and cosine drift method specifically — that's what matters for large model evaluation.
targets.notes: Less about revenue pitch, more about getting the tool used in a high-profile pipeline. If LAION uses it, credibility skyrockets.
